answer:
There are two distinct entities related to Consensus and the Model Context Protocol (MCP):

The Consensus AI Connect MCP server is a stateless, enterprise-grade intermediary that allows AI platforms to query the Consensus research database (200 million papers) via OAuth2-secured connections [1]. It acts as an enhancement layer, respecting existing RBAC permission structures without storing user data [1]. You can integrate it into projects using the following command [2]:

```bash
npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-consensus -s
```

Separately, the @consensus-tools/mcp package is an adapter for consensus-tools that exposes agent management, job lifecycles, and workflow execution as MCP tools, resources, and prompts for LLM agents [3][4]. You install it via npm or pnpm [3][5]:

```json
"mcpServers": {
  "consensus-tools": {
    "command": "npx",
    "args": ["@consensus-tools/mcp"]
  }
}
```
